sources.crime: Crime Statistics Agency Victoria — criminal incidents by suburb/town (LGA fallback), person vs property split, YE Mar 2026
sources.housing: ABS Census 2021 G37 (tenure x dwelling structure) by SA2
sources.demographics: ABS Census 2021 G01 (age 0-14 child share) by SA2
sources.prices: Victorian Valuer-General — Median House/Unit by Suburb time series (2014-2024)
sources.rents: DFFH Rental Report — moving annual median rents by suburb (LGA fallback), Sep 2025
sources.transport: DTP annual train-station patronage (metro + V/Line) station locations, FY2024-25
sources.schools: Vic Dept of Education — School Locations 2025 (all sectors)
sources.zoning: Vicmap Planning / VicPlan — planning-scheme zones + Heritage Overlay (sampled per SA2)
sources.electricity: Geoscience Australia — National Electricity Infrastructure (transmission lines + substations, v4 2024)

206011106.name: Brunswick East
206011106.explanation_live: Lower-than-average personal-crime rates, with a high socio-economic profile (SEIFA decile 10). Property crime runs higher here, typically retail/transport precincts rather than homes. Northcote station is ~1.55 km away.
206011106.explanation_dev: only ~24% detached houses, so it's already fairly built-up. 53% of land sits under protective zoning (NRZ-led), which caps redevelopment, and 14% is under a Heritage Overlay. Grid capacity remains a proxy (see Infrastructure).
206011106.explanation_invest: Median house $1.35M (2024): -3.6% over 12 months, -2.1% p.a. over 3 years — soft/flat recent growth. Mid-market pricing.
206011106.explanation_infra: Strong grid support: ~0.35 km from a transmission line (66 kV); 36 substations within 10 km, nearest ~0.72 km. Well placed for larger-scale development, subdivision or future EV/charging clusters.
